title: Project Feedback: A River Restoration Study

turns.speaker: Dr Farrell
turns.text: Let's go through the feedback on your river restoration project. Overall it's strong, but I've got a few things to raise. Hannah, can you summarise what you set out to measure?

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Sure. We wanted to see whether re-planting trees along the riverbank changed the water quality downstream. So we compared a restored stretch with a bare stretch further up.

turns.speaker: Deepak
turns.text: And we measured three things at each site — the water temperature, the amount of sediment, and the number of insect species, which is a good indicator of overall health.

turns.speaker: Dr Farrell
turns.text: Good design. Now, your temperature results were convincing — the shaded, restored stretch was clearly cooler. But I wasn't persuaded by the sediment data. Why not?

turns.speaker: Deepak
turns.text: I think it's because we only sampled once, after heavy rain. The storm probably washed a lot of extra soil in, so that single reading isn't reliable.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: I'd actually push back slightly there. I don't think it was only the rain. I suspect our sediment method was too crude — we just eyeballed how cloudy the water looked instead of using proper equipment.

turns.speaker: Dr Farrell
turns.text: That's a fair challenge, Hannah, and I lean towards your view. A subjective judgement of cloudiness isn't defensible in a report. Next time, borrow the turbidity meter from the lab. It gives you a real number.

turns.speaker: Deepak
turns.text: Fair enough. I still think the timing didn't help, but I agree the method was the bigger weakness.

turns.speaker: Dr Farrell
turns.text: The insect count, on the other hand, was excellent — that's the standout part of the project. What did you find?

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: The restored stretch had nearly double the number of species. That was the clearest result of all, and it really supports the idea that the planting is working.

turns.speaker: Dr Farrell
turns.text: It does. Now, for the write-up, let's split the revisions. Someone needs to redo the sediment section, someone needs to build the final charts, and I'll rewrite the feedback on your statistics.

turns.speaker: Deepak
turns.text: I'll take on the sediment section, since I collected most of that data. I'd like the chance to fix it properly.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: And I'll produce the charts — I enjoy the visual side, and I've already got the insect data laid out neatly.

turns.speaker: Dr Farrell
turns.text: Perfect. And I'll handle the statistics commentary so it's consistent. One firm deadline: I want the revised draft submitted by Friday of week ten. Don't leave it to the last minute.

turns.speaker: Hannah
turns.text: Week ten, understood. Thanks — it's really helpful to know which parts are solid and which need work.

questionGroups.prompt: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questionGroups.instructions_html: What was the students' project trying to find out?

options.text: whether planting trees changed downstream water quality
how quickly trees grow beside a river
which insects live in restored rivers

questionGroups.prompt: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questionGroups.instructions_html: Why does the tutor prefer Hannah's explanation of the weak sediment data?

options.text: because the sampling happened after rain
because judging cloudiness by eye is not defensible
because too few sites were sampled

questions.prompt_text: Dr Farrell agrees the main weakness was that

questionGroups.instructions_html: Which TWO parts of the project does the tutor praise?

options.text: the choice of a rainy sampling day

questions.prompt_text: Two strengths praised by the tutor

questionGroups.prompt: Who will take on each revision task?
questionGroups.instructions_html: Choose your answer from the list of people A–C and select the correct letter for each task.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.prompt_text: Next time, they should measure sediment using a ______ meter.
The restored stretch had nearly ______ the number of insect species.
The revised draft must be submitted by Friday of week ______.
